paper_title: The effect of environmental accounting information disclosure on financial performance of Vietnamese listed industrial firms: The moderating role of Leverage and Big4

entities.description: Reporting data from firms' environmental activities, resource utilization, and environmental protection actions.
The measure of how well a firm can use assets from its primary mode of business and generate revenues.
Theory stating organizations must abide by societal values to win community support.
Theory suggesting a corporation should address the needs and interests of various groups.
Theory stating that disclosing information voluntarily boosts financial performance.
The use of borrowed capital for an investment, acting as a moderating variable in this study.
Refers to the four largest professional services networks in the world, acting as a quality moderator for financial statements.
When the same person holds both the CEO and chairperson positions.
A non-probability sampling technique used to select the study population.
Technique used to calculate the unweighted disclosure index for EAID from annual reports.
A type of linear least squares method for estimating the unknown parameters in a linear regression model.
A statistical model in which the model parameters are fixed or non-random quantities.
A statistical model where the model parameters are socialized as random variables.
A method used to estimate the parameters of a linear regression model when there is a certain degree of correlation between the residuals.
Secondary data comprising 425 observations from 85 industrial listed firms on the Ho Chi Minh Stock Exchange.
The average level of environmental accounting information disclosure in the sample.
The mean Return on Assets of the sampled firms.
The mean Return on Equity of the sampled firms.
The average duration the firms have been listed on the stock market.
The average debt-to-equity or debt-to-assets ratio of the firms.
The percentage of listed companies using Big 4 auditing firms.
The percentage of firms where the CEO is also the Chairman.
Research field focusing on accounting practices related to environmental impact and corporate financial performance.
